spec_match.rationale: Accurately conveys the core technical difference between sequential re-buffering head-of-line blocking and Chrome 150's out-of-order streams dispatch.

interactive.rationale: The page is entirely static and contains no interactive elements or executable JS logic, violating the showcase rule that concept pages must be genuinely interactive.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Perfect alignment with the parent description of explaining ordering differences.

edge_cases.rationale: Conceptual breakdown of retransmission packets and network buffer queues is clear.

references.rationale: Cites the correct ChromeStatus tracking link under see also.

design_system.rationale: Structure is visually consistent, employing neat border tracks and standard flex columns.

openQuestions.title: Convert static pipeline grid to an interactive simulator
openQuestions.detail: The page currently presents a completely static visual comparison. Build an interactive timeline playground: let users click 'Delay Chunk' buttons to adjust simulated arrival latency, select in-order or out-of-order modes, and click 'Play' to watch chunks animate through buffer queues and render in real-time.
